af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Financial data aggregation and bank connectivity platform. auth_complexity_score=40 (very complex) — two-tier auth: Client ID + Secret + per-user access tokens obtained via Plaid Link UI flow. Programmatic bank connection without Link UI is not possible. Access tokens never expire but ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED errors require user re-authentication. OpenAPI spec available. Retry guidance documented. Institution coverage varies — small banks may use scraped data.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory for financial data. Two-tier auth with per-user access tokens and product-level scoping (transactions, auth, identity, balance). SOC2 Type II, PCI-DSS, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Webhook verification uses JWT with Plaid's public keys. Client secrets and access tokens via environment variables. Industry-leading security posture for financial data aggregation.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 with SLA. Stable versioned API with OpenAPI spec. Retry guidance documented. Transactions are asynchronous — polling or webhooks required. Webhook delivery includes retry handling. Institution reliability varies by data source quality. Mature platform with strong uptime track record.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. MySQL-compatible serverless database with Git-like schema branching. Service tokens with granular per-resource permission scoping (excellent design). No free tier since April 2024 — minimum ~$39/month. Full branching workflow required for schema changes: create branch → DDL → deploy request → merge. Foreign key constraints disabled at Vitess layer. No official SDK — use raw HTTP or generate from OpenAPI spec.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Service tokens with exceptional scope granularity: create_databases, read_branch, create_branch, connect_production_branch, create_deploy_requests, etc. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Granular enough for least-privilege agent access. Tokens via Authorization header/env vars. Vitess-backed database with enterprise reliability.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 with SLA. Stable API with OpenAPI spec. Deploy requests are async — agents must poll status or use webhooks. Schema linting failures require DDL reformulation. Rows read/written billing model means read-heavy agents can generate unexpected costs. Reliable managed Vitess infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Comprehensive US financial market data (stocks, options, forex, crypto) with REST + WebSocket APIs and SDKs. Simple API key as query parameter or header. Free tier limited to 5 req/min — nearly unusable for agents. Real-time vs delayed data access depends on plan level. OCC symbology required for options. WebSocket streams need reconnection logic. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key per account — no fine-grained scopes. SEC/FINRA regulated data source. No sensitive personal data handled — market data only. API key via environment variables. Regulated financial data context means strong operational security practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable API with good backwards compatibility. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. WebSocket streams require reconnection logic. Market data SLA during trading hours is critical — generally reliable.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (community-maintained, may not cover all endpoints). Open-source product analytics with feature flags, session replay, A/B testing. Personal API key for management, project API key for public event ingestion. Feature flag evaluation requires distinct_id matching user identity system. HogQL has limitations vs standard SQL. Event ingestion is asynchronous. Self-hosted versions may drift from cloud API behavior.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Personal API key grants full access (no fine-grained scopes). Project API key for event ingestion can be public (appropriate for client-side tracking). SOC2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA compliant. Open source with active security maintenance. Secrets via environment variables.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2 with SLA for cloud. Stable API with OpenAPI spec. Self-hosted instances need manual updates and can drift from cloud behavior. Retry guidance documented. Event ingestion is async — freshly sent events not immediately queryable. Cursor-based pagination.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Management API for Postman platform (collections, environments, workspaces, monitors, mock servers). Simple API key authentication. API key compromise exposes all workspaces — no scope granularity. Collection UIDs required for most operations. Rate limit of 60 req/min easy to hit iterating large workspaces. Fork/pull request workflows are UI-only — no API support for collection version control.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API keys grant full account access with no scope granularity — compromise risk. Team access managed via workspace sharing (coarse-grained). SOC2 and GDPR compliant. API key via environment variables. Collections may contain API keys in environment variables — careful handling required.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable API. Some operations paid-plan-only. Cursor-based pagination. Retry guidance minimal. 60 req/min rate limit requires agent throttling. Generally reliable platform for API development tooling.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. High-deliverability transactional email API. Excellent documentation. Simple API token in X-Postmark-Server-Token header. Test mode emails never delivered — easy to forget switching to production. Marketing content strictly prohibited — accounts can be suspended. Message streams separate transactional from broadcast. Domain DKIM/SPF setup required for production. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Server tokens (per-server) and Account tokens (global) provide two levels of access control. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Clean API token via X-Postmark-Server-Token header. No OAuth complexity. Excellent security posture for transactional email — purpose-built and focused.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2 with SLA. Stable mature API. Retry guidance documented. High-deliverability focus makes reliability a core business requirement. Bounce and complaint webhooks for delivery monitoring. Test mode/production stream distinction requires care.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 62). Agentic PowerPoint generation from documents/research. auth_complexity_score=45 — requires multiple API keys: MinerU (expires every 14 days), Tavily (web search), plus an LLM endpoint. 14-day MinerU key expiry is a significant operational burden for automated agents. Context overflow during long generation tasks is a documented concern. Docker Compose required for containerized deployment. Error handling is basic.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for outbound LLM API calls. Multiple API keys required from different providers — no unified auth. MinerU 14-day key expiry requires frequent credential rotation. API keys via .env file (acceptable but vault preferred). Community project — verify all dependencies before use. No formal security audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on three external APIs simultaneously (MinerU, Tavily, LLM). Any single service outage breaks generation. MinerU key expiry causes silent auth failures if not rotated. Context overflow during long tasks is a documented issue. Early project with limited reliability track record.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Python-native workflow orchestration with REST API, OpenAPI spec, and SDKs. Workspace-scoped API keys and service accounts for automation. Flow runs are async — agents must poll UUID or use webhooks. Stack locks prevent concurrent deployments. Prefect 2.x vs 3.x have breaking API differences — verify version compatibility. Flow cancellation is cooperative (running tasks may not stop immediately).

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Workspace-scoped API keys with service accounts for automation. SOC2 certified. Role-based access within workspaces. API key via PREFECT_API_KEY env var. Secrets management integration available. Self-hosted vs Prefect Cloud URL must be configured correctly.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Prefect Cloud has status page and SOC2. Prefect 2.x/3.x breaking differences are a real migration concern. Flow run IDs (UUIDs) must be stored for status polling. Retry guidance documented. Cooperative cancellation means cleanup isn't immediate. Offset-based pagination.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Self-hosted PHP e-commerce platform with Webservice API. API key in HTTP Basic Auth (key as username, empty password) or X-Auth-Key header with per-resource scopes. Returns XML by default — must pass output_format=JSON. Full object schema required in PUT requests (no partial updates). Webservice must be explicitly enabled in admin. No webhook support — polling required. API performance depends heavily on hosting quality.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted — TLS quality is operator responsibility. API key with HTTP Basic Auth and per-resource permission scoping configured in admin back office. GDPR compliant in default config. Open source PHP — operator must maintain updates and patches. API key in Basic Auth is less secure than header-based auth. No formal security audit process.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Mature project (20+ years) but self-hosted version lag is common. No webhooks — agents must poll for changes. Full PUT schema requirement creates retry complexity. API performance heavily tied to hosting quality. No built-in HA or failover.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Infrastructure-as-code platform with REST API, OpenAPI spec, and webhooks. Simple PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN env var auth. Deployments are async — agents must poll update status or use webhooks. Stack locks prevent concurrent deployments on same stack. Secrets encrypted at rest — write-only via API (cannot read back). Program runtime runs locally or in Pulumi Deployments, not in the API call. Stack outputs available only after successful deployment.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Personal or org access tokens with org-level scoping. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Secrets encrypted at rest and write-only — cannot be read back via API. PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN via environment variable. Open source CLI with maintained dependencies. No fine-grained per-operation permissions.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable REST API with cursor pagination. Deployments are async — polling required. Stack locks create retry complexity for concurrent agents. Resource ordering computed by engine. Freemium with no credit card required for free tier. Mature IaC platform with good reliability.
